docs.title: docx-editor
docs.text:
Pure Python library for Word document track changes and comments, without requiring Microsoft Word.

Note: The PyPI package is named docx-editor because docx-edit was too similar to an existing package.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Hash-Anchored Paragraph References: target edits with paragraph refs like P2#f3c1
  • Batch Editing: apply multiple paragraph-scoped edits with upfront hash validation
  • Paragraph Rewrite: rewrite a paragraph and generate tracked changes from the diff
  • Track Changes: Replace, delete, and insert text with revision tracking
  • Comments: Add, reply, resolve, and delete comments
  • Revision Management: List, accept, and reject tracked changes
  • Cross-Boundary Editing: Find and replace text spanning multiple XML elements
  • Cross-Platform: Works on Linux, macOS, and Windows
  • No Dependencies: Only requires defusedxml for secure XML parsing

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install docx-editor

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from docx_editor import Document

with Document.open("contract.docx", author="Legal Team") as doc:
    # List paragraphs to get hash-anchored references.
    for paragraph in doc.list_paragraphs():
        print(paragraph)
    # Example output:
    # P1#a7b2| Introduction to the contract...
    # P2#f3c1| Payment is due within 30 days...

    # Edit methods require a paragraph ref and return the updated ref.
    ref = doc.replace("30 days", "60 days", paragraph="P2#f3c1")
    ref = doc.insert_after("Payment", " terms", paragraph=ref)
    doc.delete("obsolete text", paragraph="P5#d4e5")

    # Comments and revision management.
    doc.add_comment("Section 5", "Please review")
    revisions = doc.list_revisions()
    if revisions:
        doc.accept_revision(revisions[0].id)

    doc.save()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
from docx_editor import Document

with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    ref = doc.list_paragraphs()[0].split("|", 1)[0]
    doc.replace("old term", "new term", paragraph=ref)
    doc.save()
# Automatically closes and cleans up workspace

docs.title: Document
docs.text:
The main entry point for docx-editor. Provides methods for opening documents, making tracked changes, managing comments, and handling revisions.

from docx_editor import Document

docs.title: Document.open(path, author=None, force_recreate=False)
docs.text:
Open a Word document for editing.

Parameters:

  • path (str | Path): Path to the .docx file
  • author (str, optional): Author name for tracked changes. Defaults to system username.
  • force_recreate (bool): If True, delete any existing workspace (stale or in-sync) before opening — any unsaved edits in the workspace are discarded. Use this to recover from WorkspaceSyncError. Defaults to False.

Returns: Document instance ready for editing

Raises:

  • WorkspaceSyncError: If the source .docx was modified since the workspace was created. Pass force_recreate=True to discard the stale workspace and re-unpack from the current source. The workspace is never deleted silently.

Example:

doc = Document.open("contract.docx")
doc = Document.open("contract.docx", author="Legal Team")

docs.title: author
docs.text:
Get the author name for tracked changes.

print(doc.author)  # "Legal Team"

docs.title: source_path
docs.text:
Get the path to the source document.

print(doc.source_path)  # Path("/path/to/contract.docx")

docs.title: list_paragraphs(max_chars=80)
docs.text:
List paragraphs with hash-anchored references.

Parameters:

  • max_chars (int): Maximum preview length. Use 0 for refs only.

Returns: List of strings in the form P{index}#{hash}| preview text

Example:

refs = doc.list_paragraphs()
for ref in refs:
    print(ref)

docs.title: get_visible_text()
docs.text:
Get flattened visible document text. Inserted text is included and deleted text is excluded.

Returns: Visible text with paragraphs separated by newlines (str)

Example:

text = doc.get_visible_text()

docs.title: find_text(text, occurrence=0)
docs.text:
Find text in the document, including text spanning XML element boundaries.

Parameters:

  • text (str): Text to search for
  • occurrence (int): Which occurrence to return. Defaults to 0.

Returns: Match information, or None if not found

Example:

match = doc.find_text("Aim: To")
if match and match.spans_boundary:
    print("Text spans multiple XML contexts")

docs.title: count_matches(text)
docs.text:
Count visible text matches across the document.

Parameters:

  • text (str): Text to search for

Returns: Number of occurrences found (int)

Example:

if doc.count_matches("Section 5") > 1:
    print("Use paragraph refs and occurrence to target the intended match")

docs.title: replace(find, replace_with, *, paragraph, occurrence=0)
docs.text:
Replace text with tracked changes.

Parameters:

  • find (str): Text to find and replace
  • replace_with (str): Replacement text
  • paragraph (str): Paragraph reference from list_paragraphs(), such as P2#f3c1
  • occurrence (int): Which occurrence within the paragraph. Defaults to 0.

Returns: Updated paragraph reference (str)

Example:

ref = doc.replace("30 days", "60 days", paragraph="P2#f3c1")
doc.replace("net", "gross", paragraph=ref)

docs.title: delete(text, *, paragraph, occurrence=0)
docs.text:
Mark text as deleted with tracked changes.

Parameters:

  • text (str): Text to mark as deleted
  • paragraph (str): Paragraph reference from list_paragraphs(), such as P2#f3c1
  • occurrence (int): Which occurrence within the paragraph. Defaults to 0.

Returns: Updated paragraph reference (str)

Example:

ref = doc.delete("obsolete clause", paragraph="P5#d4e5")

docs.title: insert_after(anchor, text, *, paragraph, occurrence=0)
docs.text:
Insert text after anchor with tracked changes.

Parameters:

  • anchor (str): Text to find as insertion point
  • text (str): Text to insert after the anchor
  • paragraph (str): Paragraph reference from list_paragraphs(), such as P2#f3c1
  • occurrence (int): Which occurrence within the paragraph. Defaults to 0.

Returns: Updated paragraph reference (str)

Example:

ref = doc.insert_after("Section 5", " (as amended)", paragraph="P3#b2c4")

docs.title: insert_before(anchor, text, *, paragraph, occurrence=0)
docs.text:
Insert text before anchor with tracked changes.

Parameters:

  • anchor (str): Text to find as insertion point
  • text (str): Text to insert before the anchor
  • paragraph (str): Paragraph reference from list_paragraphs(), such as P2#f3c1
  • occurrence (int): Which occurrence within the paragraph. Defaults to 0.

Returns: Updated paragraph reference (str)

Example:

ref = doc.insert_before("Section 6", "New clause: ", paragraph="P4#a7b2")

docs.title: rewrite_paragraph(ref, new_text)
docs.text:
Rewrite a paragraph using tracked changes generated from a word-level diff.

Parameters:

  • ref (str): Paragraph reference from list_paragraphs()
  • new_text (str): Desired paragraph text

Returns: Updated paragraph reference (str)

Example:

ref = doc.rewrite_paragraph("P2#f3c1", "Payment is due within 60 days after invoice receipt.")

docs.title: batch_edit(operations)
docs.text:
Apply multiple edits after validating paragraph hashes up front.

Parameters:

  • operations (list[EditOperation]): Edit operations to apply

Returns: Updated paragraph references in input order (list[str])

Example:

from docx_editor import EditOperation

new_refs = doc.batch_edit([
    EditOperation(action="replace", find="old", replace_with="new", paragraph="P2#f3c1"),
    EditOperation(action="delete", text="remove this", paragraph="P5#d4e5"),
])

docs.title: batch_rewrite(rewrites)
docs.text:
Rewrite multiple paragraphs after validating paragraph hashes up front.

Parameters:

  • rewrites (list[tuple[str, str]]): Pairs of paragraph ref and desired text

Returns: Updated paragraph references in input order (list[str])

Example:

new_refs = doc.batch_rewrite([
    ("P1#a7b2", "Updated first paragraph."),
    ("P3#c3d4", "Updated third paragraph."),
])

docs.title: add_comment(anchor_text, comment)
docs.text:
Add a comment anchored to specific text.

Parameters:

  • anchor_text (str): Text to attach the comment to
  • comment (str): The comment content

Returns: The comment ID (int)

Example:

doc.add_comment("Section 5", "Please review this section")

docs.title: reply_to_comment(comment_id, reply)
docs.text:
Add a reply to an existing comment.

Parameters:

  • comment_id (int): ID of the comment to reply to
  • reply (str): The reply content

Returns: The new comment ID for the reply (int)

Example:

doc.reply_to_comment(0, "I agree with this change")

docs.title: list_comments(author=None)
docs.text:
List all comments in the document.

Parameters:

  • author (str, optional): If provided, filter by author name

Returns: List of Comment objects (with replies nested)

Example:

comments = doc.list_comments()
for c in comments:
    print(f"{c.author}: {c.text}")

docs.title: resolve_comment(comment_id)
docs.text:
Mark a comment as resolved.

Parameters:

  • comment_id (int): ID of the comment to resolve

Returns: True if resolved, False if not found (bool)

Example:

doc.resolve_comment(0)

docs.title: delete_comment(comment_id)
docs.text:
Delete a comment from the document.

Parameters:

  • comment_id (int): ID of the comment to delete

Returns: True if deleted, False if not found (bool)

Example:

doc.delete_comment(0)

docs.title: list_revisions(author=None)
docs.text:
List all tracked changes in the document.

Parameters:

  • author (str, optional): If provided, filter by author name

Returns: List of Revision objects

Example:

revisions = doc.list_revisions()
for r in revisions:
    print(f"{r.type}: {r.text} by {r.author}")

docs.title: accept_revision(revision_id)
docs.text:
Accept a revision by ID.

  • For insertions: keeps the inserted content
  • For deletions: permanently removes the deleted content

Parameters:

  • revision_id (int): ID of the revision to accept

Returns: True if accepted, False if not found (bool)

Example:

doc.accept_revision(1)

docs.title: reject_revision(revision_id)
docs.text:
Reject a revision by ID.

  • For insertions: removes the inserted content
  • For deletions: restores the deleted content

Parameters:

  • revision_id (int): ID of the revision to reject

Returns: True if rejected, False if not found (bool)

Example:

doc.reject_revision(1)

docs.title: accept_all(author=None)
docs.text:
Accept all revisions.

Parameters:

  • author (str, optional): If provided, only accept revisions by this author

Returns: Number of revisions accepted (int)

Example:

count = doc.accept_all()
print(f"Accepted {count} revisions")

docs.title: reject_all(author=None)
docs.text:
Reject all revisions.

Parameters:

  • author (str, optional): If provided, only reject revisions by this author

Returns: Number of revisions rejected (int)

Example:

count = doc.reject_all(author="OtherUser")

docs.title: save(path=None, validate=False)
docs.text:
Save the document.

Parameters:

  • path (str | Path, optional): Output path. Defaults to original source path.
  • validate (bool): If True, validate with LibreOffice before saving. Defaults to False.

Returns: Path to the saved document (Path)

Example:

doc.save()  # Save to original path
doc.save("contract_v2.docx")  # Save to new path

docs.title: close(cleanup=True)
docs.text:
Close the document and clean up workspace.

Parameters:

  • cleanup (bool): If True, delete the workspace folder. Defaults to True.

Example:

doc.close()  # Clean up workspace
doc.close(cleanup=False)  # Keep workspace for inspection

docs.title: Comment
docs.text:
Represents a document comment.

from docx_editor import Comment

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description id int The comment ID text str The comment content author str The comment author date datetime or None When the comment was created resolved bool Whether the comment is resolved replies list[Comment] Nested replies to this comment

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
comments = doc.list_comments()
for comment in comments:
    print(f"ID: {comment.id}")
    print(f"Text: {comment.text}")
    print(f"Author: {comment.author}")
    print(f"Date: {comment.date}")
    print(f"Resolved: {comment.resolved}")
    print(f"Replies: {len(comment.replies)}")

docs.title: Revision
docs.text:
Represents a tracked change (insertion or deletion).

from docx_editor import Revision

docs.title: Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Type Description id int The revision ID type str Either "insertion" or "deletion" author str The revision author date datetime or None When the revision was made text str The inserted or deleted text

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
revisions = doc.list_revisions()
for rev in revisions:
    symbol = "+" if rev.type == "insertion" else "-"
    print(f"{symbol} {rev.text} (by {rev.author})")

docs.title: TextNotFoundError
docs.text:
Raised when the specified text is not found in the document.

from docx_editor.exceptions import TextNotFoundError

try:
    doc.replace("nonexistent text", "new text", paragraph="P2#f3c1")
except TextNotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Text not found: {e}")

docs.title: CommentError
docs.text:
Raised when a comment operation fails.

from docx_editor.exceptions import CommentError

try:
    doc.reply_to_comment(999, "reply")
except CommentError as e:
    print(f"Comment error: {e}")

docs.title: RevisionError
docs.text:
Raised when a revision operation fails.

from docx_editor.exceptions import RevisionError

docs.title: WorkspaceExistsError
docs.text:
Raised when attempting to create a workspace that already exists.

from docx_editor.exceptions import WorkspaceExistsError

docs.title: WorkspaceSyncError
docs.text:
Raised when the workspace is out of sync with the source document.

from docx_editor.exceptions import WorkspaceSyncError

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide covers the essential usage patterns for docx-editor.

docs.title: Opening Documents
docs.text:
from docx_editor import Document

doc = Document.open("contract.docx", author="Legal Team")
# ... make changes ...
doc.save()
doc.close()

The recommended approach is to use a context manager so the temporary workspace is cleaned up automatically:

from docx_editor import Document

with Document.open("contract.docx", author="Legal Team") as doc:
    # ... make changes ...
    doc.save()

If the source .docx was modified outside this library since the workspace was created, Document.open() raises WorkspaceSyncError instead of silently discarding the workspace. Pass force_recreate=True to acknowledge the divergence and re-unpack from the current source:

doc = Document.open("contract.docx", force_recreate=True)

docs.title: Paragraph References
docs.text:
Tracked edit methods are scoped to a hash-anchored paragraph reference. Start by listing paragraphs:

with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    for paragraph in doc.list_paragraphs():
        print(paragraph)

Output looks like:

P1#a7b2| Introduction to the contract...
P2#f3c1| Payment is due within 30 days...
P3#b2c4| Section 5 describes review obligations...

Use the P{index}#{hash} part as the paragraph= argument. Edit methods return a new paragraph ref after the hash changes, so keep the returned value when chaining edits in the same paragraph.

docs.title: Replace Text
docs.text:
with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    ref = "P2#f3c1"
    ref = doc.replace("30 days", "60 days", paragraph=ref)
    doc.save()

This creates a tracked deletion of 30 days and a tracked insertion of 60 days.

docs.title: Delete Text
docs.text:
with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    ref = "P5#d4e5"
    doc.delete("obsolete clause", paragraph=ref)
    doc.save()

docs.title: Insert Text
docs.text:
with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    ref = "P3#b2c4"
    ref = doc.insert_after("Section 5", " (as amended)", paragraph=ref)
    doc.insert_before("review", "legal ", paragraph=ref)
    doc.save()

docs.title: Rewrite a Paragraph
docs.text:
with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    ref = "P2#f3c1"
    doc.rewrite_paragraph(ref, "Payment is due within 60 days after invoice receipt.")
    doc.save()

rewrite_paragraph() compares the current paragraph text to the desired text and creates tracked changes from the diff.

docs.title: Batch Edits
docs.text:
from docx_editor import Document, EditOperation

with Document.open("contract.docx") as doc:
    new_refs = doc.batch_edit([
        EditOperation(action="replace", find="30 days", replace_with="60 days", paragraph="P2#f3c1"),
        EditOperation(action="delete", text="obsolete clause", paragraph="P5#d4e5"),
        EditOperation(action="insert_after", anchor="Section 5", text=" (as amended)", paragraph="P3#b2c4"),
    ])
    print(new_refs)
    doc.save()

docs.title: Add a Comment
docs.text:
comment_id = doc.add_comment("Section 5", "Please review this section")
print(f"Created comment with ID: {comment_id}")

docs.title: Reply to a Comment
docs.text: reply_id = doc.reply_to_comment(comment_id=0, reply="I agree with this change")

docs.title: List Comments
docs.text:
comments = doc.list_comments()
for comment in comments:
    print(f"Comment {comment.id}: {comment.text}")
    print(f"  Author: {comment.author}")
    print(f"  Resolved: {comment.resolved}")

    for reply in comment.replies:
        print(f"  Reply: {reply.text}")

Filter by author:

my_comments = doc.list_comments(author="Legal Team")

docs.title: Resolve or Delete a Comment
docs.text:
doc.resolve_comment(comment_id=0)
doc.delete_comment(comment_id=0)

docs.title: List Revisions
docs.text:
revisions = doc.list_revisions()
for rev in revisions:
    print(f"{rev.type}: '{rev.text}' by {rev.author}")

Filter by author:

my_revisions = doc.list_revisions(author="Legal Team")

docs.title: Accept or Reject a Revision
docs.text:
# For insertions, accept keeps the inserted content.
# For deletions, accept permanently removes the deleted content.
doc.accept_revision(revision_id=1)

# For insertions, reject removes the inserted content.
# For deletions, reject restores the deleted content.
doc.reject_revision(revision_id=1)

docs.title: Accept or Reject All
docs.text:
count = doc.accept_all()
print(f"Accepted {count} revisions")

count = doc.reject_all(author="OtherUser")
print(f"Rejected {count} revisions")

docs.title: Saving and Closing
docs.text:
doc.save()                  # Save to original path
doc.save("contract_v2.docx") # Save to a new path
doc.close()                 # Delete workspace
doc.close(cleanup=False)    # Keep workspace for inspection

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
from docx_editor import Document

with Document.open("contract.docx", author="Legal Review") as doc:
    for paragraph in doc.list_paragraphs():
        print(paragraph)

    payment_ref = doc.replace("30 days", "60 days", paragraph="P2#f3c1")
    doc.insert_after("payment terms", " (net 60)", paragraph=payment_ref)
    doc.delete("penalty clause", paragraph="P7#a10b")

    doc.add_comment("Section 5", "Needs legal review")

    for rev in doc.list_revisions():
        print(f"{rev.type}: {rev.text}")

    doc.accept_all(author="Senior Counsel")
    doc.save()
